title: Rank-preserving calibrated stacking rather than raw-probability averaging
summary: Scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration is strictly monotonic and therefore preserves each model’s ranking, unlike isotonic which can change AUC through ties.
rationale: Scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration is strictly monotonic and therefore preserves each model’s ranking, unlike isotonic which can change AUC through ties. For an AUC task, that makes sigmoid-calibrated OOF predictions attractive as safer stacker inputs than raw probabilities from heterogeneous specialists.
implementation_hint: For each base model, fit fold-wise Platt/sigmoid calibration on OOF scores, then train a very small meta-learner on calibrated OOF columns plus a few regime indicators (`wet`, `late-race`, `pre-season`, `compound class`). Compare against simple mean/rank averaging; keep isotonic only if it materially helps blend diversity.
expected_effect: Can improve ensemble robustness and public generalization by aligning score scales without destroying within-model ordering.
risk: Calibration alone does not create signal; gains depend on having genuinely different specialists. Overfit meta-learners can erase the benefit.
